Big.translation_notes: Big.js uses method chaining (.plus, .minus, .mul, .div). Python Decimal uses operator overloading (+, -, *, /). Convert all method calls to operators. Also handle .toNumber() -> float(), .toFixed(n) -> format with precision, .eq(x) -> == x, .gt(x) -> > x, .gte(x) -> >= x, .lt(x) -> < x, .lte(x) -> <= x, .abs() -> abs()

differenceInDays.translation_notes: Implement as: def difference_in_days(end, start): return (end - start).days

eachDayOfInterval.translation_notes: Implement as generator: def each_day_of_interval(interval, step=1): current = interval['start']; end = interval['end']; while current <= end: yield current; current += timedelta(days=step)

eachYearOfInterval.translation_notes: Implement as generator yielding first day of each year in interval

format.translation_notes: Map date-fns format tokens to strftime: yyyy->%Y, MM->%m, dd->%d, etc. Implement: def format_date(date, format_str): # convert date-fns format to strftime

isAfter.translation_notes: Direct translation: isAfter(a, b) -> a > b

isBefore.translation_notes: Direct translation: isBefore(a, b) -> a < b

isWithinInterval.translation_notes: Implement as: def is_within_interval(date, interval): return interval['start'] <= date <= interval['end']

startOfDay.translation_notes: Implement as: def start_of_day(date): return date.replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)

endOfDay.translation_notes: Implement as: def end_of_day(date): return date.replace(hour=23, minute=59, second=59, microsecond=999999)

startOfYear.translation_notes: Implement as: def start_of_year(date): return date.replace(month=1, day=1, hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)

endOfYear.translation_notes: Implement as: def end_of_year(date): return date.replace(month=12, day=31, hour=23, minute=59, second=59, microsecond=999999)

subDays.translation_notes: Implement as: def sub_days(date, days): return date - timedelta(days=days)

addMilliseconds.translation_notes: Implement as: def add_milliseconds(date, ms): return date + timedelta(milliseconds=ms)

isThisYear.translation_notes: Implement as: def is_this_year(date): return date.year == datetime.now().year

isNumber.translation_notes: Direct translation: isNumber(x) -> isinstance(x, (int, float, Decimal))

sortBy.translation_notes: sortBy(arr, fn) -> sorted(arr, key=fn). If fn is a string property name, use key=lambda x: x[fn] or key=lambda x: getattr(x, fn)

sum.translation_notes: Use Python builtin sum(). Note: for Decimal, may need sum(values, Decimal(0)) to maintain precision

uniqBy.translation_notes: Implement as: def uniq_by(items, key_fn): seen = set(); result = []; for item in items: k = key_fn(item) if callable(key_fn) else item[key_fn]; if k not in seen: seen.add(k); result.append(item); return result

plainToClass.translation_notes: For dataclasses: MyClass(**plain_dict). For regular classes, may need custom constructor. Context-dependent translation.

Logger.translation_notes: Replace Logger.debug() with logging.debug(), Logger.warn() with logging.warning(), etc. Create module-level logger: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

getSum.translation_notes: Implement wrapper around sum() that handles Decimals properly

parseDate.translation_notes: Parse date strings to datetime objects, handling multiple formats

@LogPerformance.translation_notes: Implement decorator that logs execution time. Use functools.wraps. Can use logging module or print statements.
